item.title: Govt decides to honour Mohon Raihan amid criticism for dropping his name
item.description: Ministry of Cultural Affairs confirmed that they would confer the Bangla Academy Literary Award 2025 on poet Mohon Raihan on Monday, following criticism over the initial decision of dropping his name from the award list....

item.title: Yunus leaves Jamuna residence
item.description: Immediate past interim government chief adviser Muhammad Yunus on Saturday left the state guest house Jamuna, his official residence during his tenure, and moved to his private home in the capital’s Gulshan area....

item.title: Jamaat calls executed 1971 war convicts ‘martyrs’
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman on Saturday described executed party leaders who were tried and convicted for crimes against humanity committed during the 1971 War of Independence as ‘honoured martyr’....

item.title: Civilians killed in Pakistan air strikes: Afghanistan
item.description: Afghanistan accused Pakistan of killing civilians in rural Kandahar on Saturday, as deadly violence flared between the South Asian neighbours....

item.title: PM seeks officials’ cooperation to implement polls pledges
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday sought the cooperation of government officials and employees to implement the commitments made in Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s election manifesto, including those in health and education, women’s education......

item.title: BPC dismisses immediate impact
item.description: The Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation is monitoring the coordinated strikes across Iran by the United States and Israel on Saturday, triggering tensions among fuel oil traders....

item.title: War in Ukraine at four
item.description: IT IS old hat for those who know a bit about peace that it cannot be created by looking only at the various types of violence employed. The world’s focus on the Ukrainian battlefield and its characteristic mixing up ceasefire with peace is misplaced......

item.title: Extortionists fire shots at businessman’s house in Ctg
item.description: A group of miscreants allegedly fired shots at the house of a businessman in Chattogram on Saturday morning after he refused to pay money demanded in extortion....

item.title: Compliance shock for Bangladesh’s exports
item.description: THE era of securing global market access through cheap labour and aggressive pricing is drawing to a close. International trade is entering a structural phase in which competitiveness is increasingly measured not only by unit cost, but by the carbon footprint embedded in production. For Bangladesh, an economy structurally tied to export-led growth, the message from......

item.title: Examining BNP’s education manifesto
item.description: WHEN a political party assumes office, its election manifesto commitments cease to be mere pledges. They become a test of governance. Before the national elections, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party unveiled a broad and ambitious framework for education. By placing education at the centre of human capital development, social justice and economic growth, the......

item.title: Airspaces closed, flights cancelled as US-Iran war begins
item.description: Flights across the Middle East were being cancelled Saturday as several countries slammed their airspace shut after the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran....

item.title: Hidden cancer switch not talked about
item.description: WHEN most people think of cancer, they think of ‘bad genes’ damaged DNA that causes cells to grow uncontrollably. But cancer is not only about damaged genes. It is also about the system that controls the genes. There is a group of proteins that act like molecular librarians inside the cell. Their job is simple but critical. They decide which parts of the DNA are open......

item.title: Strategic silence, sacred solidarity, sovereign signalling
item.description: US AMBASSADOR to Israel Mike Huckabee set off a diplomatic firestorm in late February 2026 by saying it would ‘be fine’ if Israel ‘took it all’ between the Nile and the Euphrates. Governments across the Arab and Islamic world immediately issued a joint statement condemning the remark as ‘dangerous and inflammatory.’ Conspicuously absent, however, was......

item.title: Bangladeshis in Gulf countries asked to stay safe
item.description: The Bangladesh missions in Iraq, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait on Saturday asked Bangladeshis staying in the Gulf Arab countries, major destinations for Bangladeshi migrant workers, to stay in safe places amid attacks by the......

item.title: Govt needs to protect potato growers’ interests
item.description: WHEN favourable weather and expanded farming should strengthen rural economy in Thakurgaon, farmers of the district, instead, face severe financial losses as potato prices have declined to Tk 3–7 a kilogram, far below the production cost of Tk 12–15. The price decline has left farmers unable to recover even basic costs of seeds, fertilisers, irrigation and pesticide, pushing......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Earthquake contingency plans must be foolproof
item.description: RECURRING earthquakes of varying magnitudes require the government to revisit its preparedness and mitigation plans. A 5.4-magnitude earthquake, originating in Satkhira, was felt on February 27. With the latest event, there were nine earthquakes in February, two of which had the epicentre in Satkhira. The Department of Meteorology says that 32 earthquakes have......

item.title: Jishan, Mosaddek propel Central to final
item.description: Jishan Alam’s explosive hundred, followed by a blazing fifty from Mosaddek Hossain, propelled Central Zone to the final of the Bangladesh Cricket League one-day after they beat North Zone by two wickets at the Rajshahi Divisional Stadium on Saturday......

item.title: Bangladesh ready to beat the odds
item.description: The Bangladesh women’s national football team is putting the finishing touches on their preparation ahead of their maiden AFC Women’s Asian Cup campaign, with players confident to take up the challenge against Asia’s strongest sides......

item.title: Bangladesh should have played in World Cup: BCB director
item.description: Bangladesh should have participated in the ongoing ICC Men’s T20 World Cup, keeping aside the politics, said Bangladesh Cricket Board director Shahninan Taneem on Saturday......Daily news updates

item.title: Trump says Khamenei is dead
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Saturday that Iran’s supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was dead, after Israel and the United States launched an attack of unprecedented scale aimed at bringing down the Islamic republic....

item.title: BCL posters appear at places on DU campus
item.description: Posters of Bangladesh Chhatra League, the student wing of the Awami League, were put up on walls at several locations on the Dhaka University campus on Friday night....

item.title: Milestone students get most junior scholarships
item.description: Milestone College has achieved success in the result of the 2025 Junior Scholarship Examination....

item.title: Govt urged to reconsider decision on resuming scholarship exams
item.description: Academicians and researchers on Saturday said that the government’s decision to resume primary and junior scholarship examinations would add to the existing burden of public exams in a further boost to coaching and guidebook businesses....

item.title: Charge sheet for Prothom Alo, Star attacks in two months, says home minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Saturday said that investigators were directed to submit charge-sheets within two months in the cases filed over the attack on Prothom Alo and Daily Star....

item.title: Selim-Delwar Killing Day observed
item.description: Former student leaders on Saturday observed Selim-Delwar Killing Day in separate programmes with the vow to form unity of all anti-autocratic political organisations. ...

item.title: Drives against battery-run rickshaws intensify
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police traffic division on Saturday intensified its drives against battery-run rickshaws in an attempt to restore discipline on the main city roads....

item.title: Clinton denies wrongdoing at grilling on Epstein ties
item.description: Former US president Bill Clinton denied wrongdoing Friday to a congressional panel probing his links to Jeffrey Epstein, before calling on others to testify as Democrats seek to shift focus onto Donald Trump’s ties to the sex offender....

item.title: Man killed in clash over protesting at stalking
item.description: A 35-year-old man was killed following a violent clash between two groups of youths in Ullapara upazila of Sirajganj, reportedly ensued from protests against stalking....

item.title: Blast at India firecracker factory kills 21
item.description: An explosion at a firecracker factory in India killed at least 21 people and injured eight others on Saturday, officials told local media....

item.title: Run for UNGA presidency presents challenge: FM
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman early Saturday said that the country enjoyed strong backing from member states of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation, but faced a challenge due to the limited time available to campaign for the presidency of......

item.title: Pro-BNP lawyers in Barishal suspended court boycott
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party-backed lawyers in Barishal on Saturday announced the suspension of their court boycott program....

item.title: 24 citizens demand justice in Taqi murder case
item.description: Twenty-four citizens of the country on Saturday newly demanded justice for the murder of Narayanganj teenager Tanvir Muhammad Taqi over a decade ago....

item.title: DWASA ups ATM water price by 25pc
item.description: The price of water from Dhaka Water Supply and Sewerage Authority’s ATM booths has been increased by 25 per cent with effect from Sunday....

item.title: Workers block highway over unpaid wages
item.description: Workers of an export-oriented garment factory, B-Brothers Limited, blocked the Dhaka–Sylhet Highway at Rupganj in Narayanganj on Saturday morning, demanding unpaid wages....

item.title: Industrial workers to get paid before Eid: Muktadir
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Saturday said that outstanding salaries and allowances of the industrial workers would be paid before the forthcoming Eid-ul-Fitr, the largest religious festival of Muslims....

item.title: 15 injured in clash over ‘grabbing government land’ in Faridpur
item.description: At least 15 people were injured in a clash between two groups reportedly over occupying a government land at Harina village under Saltha in Faridpur on Friday night....

item.title: 7-year-old boy found dead in Dinajpur
item.description: A seven-year-old boy was found dead with his throat slit in a maize field at Nawabganj in Dinajpur on Friday night....

item.title: SI injured in accidental shooting at Natore police lines
item.description: A sub-inspector posted at Natore police lines was injured on Friday when a bullet from his own service pistol struck his leg....

item.title: One more Ctg gas explosion victim dies
item.description: One more burn victim from the February 23 gas explosion at a house in Chattogram’s Halishahar area died on Saturday, raising the death toll from the incident to six....

item.title: Man injured in Narinda AC explosion dies
item.description: A man died after suffering severe burns in an explosion of air-conditioner cylinders on the third floor of a five-storey building near Narinda kitchen market at Wari in Dhaka city early Friday....

item.title: Sunflowers replace tobacco in Lalmonirhat char lands
item.description: A quiet but striking transformation is unfolding on the sandy char lands of Gobardhan, on the banks of the Teesta River at Mohishkhocha union under Aditmari in Lalmonirhat....

item.title: 16 Garo families in Sylhet living in fear of eviction
item.description: Sixteen families of the national minority community, Garo, in the Khadimnagar area of Sylhet are living in fear after receiving eviction threats from a local housing company, despite having resided in the area for generations....

item.title: Pirojpur university signs deal for project plan
item.description: A formal agreement has been signed between the Pirojpur Science and Technology University authorities and the selected consulting firm for the preparation of an integrated feasibility study, master plan and related development project plan to place the......

item.title: Bangladesh expresses deep concern over safety of its citizens in Middle East
item.description: Bangladesh on Saturday expressed deep concern over the welfare of its citizens in the Middle East in the wake of recent hostilities in the region including Iran......

item.title: Highway blocked as truck kills elderly woman
item.description: A 75-year-old woman was killed by a speeding truck on the Rangpur–Dhaka Highway at Shathibari under Mithapukur in Rangpur on Friday, prompting locals to block the road for nearly four hours demanding a footbridge to allow pedestrians to cross safely....

item.title: Prime suspect, six others sent to jail over girl’s rape, murder
item.description: Seven people, including the prime accused, have been sent to jail in a case filed over the abduction, rape and murder of a 15-year-old girl in Narsingdi’s Madhabdi....

item.title: Canal digging prog undertaken to build self-reliant Bangladesh: Anee
item.description: Water resources minister Md Shahiduddin Chowdhury Anee at a programme in Sylhet on Saturday said that the canal excavation programme had been undertaken to build a self-reliant Bangladesh....

item.title: Govt working to make country self-sufficient in food: SS Tuku
item.description: The State Minister for Fisheries and Livestock, Agriculture and Food, Sultan Salauddin Tuku, said that the current government was working to make the country self-sufficient in food and to make surplus food suitable for export abroad....

item.title: Visitors, new title rise with sale
item.description: On Saturday, a public holiday featuring Shishu Prahar, visitors thronged the Amar Ekushey Book Fair while new titles also rose with the sales, said salespersons at the fairground....

item.title: NCP for democratic society free of discrimination
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Saturday celebrated its first founding anniversary with various programmes across the country, with a vow to build a discrimination-free and democratic Bangladesh....

item.title: Political parties condemn attacks on Iran
item.description: Different political parties on Saturday at separate protest programmes in Dhaka called on the world community to condemn the joint attack on Iran by the United States and Israel and force them to stop the attack....
